paper_title: Fenton's Reagent and Sediment Treatment Methods

methods.description: This method investigates the effect of Fenton's reagent on the removal of TBT, DBT, and MBT from sediments by varying the ratios of hydrogen peroxide and ferrous iron.

steps.description: Ferrous sulfate heptahydrate solutions with different concentrations in ultrapure water were added to sediments with a corresponding dry weight of 80 g and under mixing.
steps.searchable_summary: Add ferrous sulfate solutions to sediments and mix

steps.description: Exposure to light was prevented by covering the setup with aluminum foil.
steps.searchable_summary: Cover setup with aluminum foil to prevent light exposure

steps.description: The pH was adjusted to pH 3 using sulfuric acid.
steps.searchable_summary: Adjust pH to 3 using sulfuric acid

steps.description: Hydrogen peroxide was added as 6 equal aliquots at 20-min intervals.
steps.searchable_summary: Add hydrogen peroxide in 6 aliquots at 20-min intervals

steps.description: The sediment slurry was continuously mixed for 23 h and was then allowed to settle for 1 h.
steps.searchable_summary: Mix sediment slurry for 23 hours and allow to settle for 1 hour

steps.description: The supernatant was centrifuged at 4000 × G for 5 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Centrifuge supernatant at 4000 × G for 5 minutes

steps.description: Sediment was obtained from the initial setting, but also after centrifugation, and both these sediments were collected and mixed before analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect and mix sediments from initial setting and post-centrifugation

methods.description: This method describes the preparation of sediment samples, including spiking with TBT, and the analysis of TBT, DBT, and MBT content.

steps.description: A portion of the sediment was spiked with TBT to achieve a higher TBT concentration of approximately 30 mg/kg.
steps.searchable_summary: Spike sediment with TBT to achieve 30 mg/kg concentration

steps.description: Spiking was performed by adding tributyltin chloride dissolved in 10% methanol and ultra-pure water to the original sediment.
steps.searchable_summary: Add tributyltin chloride in methanol and water to sediment

steps.description: The spiked sediment was then homogenized by mixing for 3 days.
steps.searchable_summary: Homogenize spiked sediment by mixing for 3 days

steps.description: Followed by evaporation in a 40 ℃ oven for 5 days, and in a 20 ℃ fume hood for 17 days.
steps.searchable_summary: Evaporate sediment in oven and fume hood

steps.description: Spiked and original samples were sent for chemical analysis for the content of TBT, DBT, and MBT.
steps.searchable_summary: Send samples for chemical analysis of TBT, DBT, and MBT
